metadata.description: Environment name (dev, staging, prod).
Azure region for all resources.
Short project identifier used in resource names.
Globally-unique suffix (e.g. 8-char hex) to avoid naming collisions.
PostgreSQL flexible-server admin username.
PostgreSQL admin password.  Must be strong.
Docker image tag to deploy (e.g. dev-latest or git SHA).

Front Door SKU. Standard (default) is ~10x cheaper (~$35/mo vs ~$330/mo) and
supports custom WAF rules including rate limiting and bot/UA block rules.
Premium additionally enables WAF managed rule sets (OWASP DRS + Bot Manager).
We default to Standard and compensate for the missing managed rules with custom
WAF rules, a Turnstile bot challenge on auth, and app-layer hardening. Switch to
Premium for managed-signature/zero-day coverage. See docs/architecture/security.md.
Azure region.
Environment name (dev, staging, prod).
Globally unique suffix added to shared resource names.
Resource ID of the App Service that emits application telemetry.

Daily ingestion cap (GB) for the Log Analytics workspace. Once reached, the
workspace stops accepting new data for the day — protecting us from a runaway
log emitter generating thousands of dollars of ingestion. 0.2 GB/day is ~10x
the current zero-user baseline; raise it deliberately (as a reviewed Bicep
diff) if real traffic outgrows it. Default is intentionally low so the cost
floor cannot drift silently.

Log retention in days. 14 days is enough to investigate a typical incident
and triage a week-old support question, while being half the previous
30-day setting and therefore roughly half the per-GB storage cost.

properties.description: Alert when the app records more than 10 exceptions in 5 minutes.

allOf.query: union isfuzzy=true (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime)[]), AppExceptions | where TimeGenerated >= ago(5m)

properties.description: Alert when auth.login_failed custom events exceed 20 in 5 minutes.

allOf.query: union isfuzzy=true (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, Name:string)[]), AppEvents | where TimeGenerated >= ago(5m) | where Name == "auth.login_failed"

properties.description: Alert when the app records an unhandled exception or trace in the last 5 minutes.

allOf.query: union isfuzzy=true (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime)[]), AppExceptions, AppTraces | where TimeGenerated >= ago(5m) | where (tolower(tostring(column_ifexists("HandledAt", ""))) == "unhandled") or (column_ifexists("Message", "") has "Unhandled exception") or (column_ifexists("Message", "") has "uncaught exception")

metadata.description: Azure region
Globally unique ACR name (alphanumeric, 5–50 chars)
Azure region
Globally unique PostgreSQL flexible server name
Admin username
Admin password
Initial database name

Provisioned storage in GB. 20 GB is the minimum supported tier on Burstable
Flexible Server and is comfortably above current usage (well under 1 GB).
A storage increase MUST be a deliberate, reviewed Bicep change — never an
automatic upgrade — so the bill stays predictable. Raise this number only
when actual usage justifies it.

IMPORTANT: Azure Postgres Flexible Server storage can ONLY GROW in place;
it cannot be shrunk on an existing server. Lowering this value below the
server's current size will be rejected by the Azure API at deploy time. To
actually reclaim storage you must dump → recreate the server at the lower
tier → restore. The 20 GB default applies to fresh deploys; for the
existing dev server, pass the current size (or higher) from main.bicep
until you are ready to do the recreate dance.

Whether the server is allowed to silently grow its disk. Disabled so a
runaway write workload cannot quietly double the storage bill — instead it
fails loudly when disk is full, which is the signal we want at this scale.
Azure region
Globally unique Key Vault name (3–24 alphanumeric + hyphens)
Azure AD tenant ID
Azure region
Name of the App Service Plan
SKU name (B1 is cheapest for Linux containers)
Azure region
Name of the Web App
App Service Plan resource ID
ACR login server FQDN
Docker image tag to deploy
Log Analytics workspace resource ID for diagnostics
Application Insights connection string injected into the web app runtime.

ipSecurityRestrictions.name: Allow-AzureFrontDoor
ipSecurityRestrictions.description: Only accept inbound traffic from the Azure Front Door edge.

metadata.description: Globally unique WAF policy name (alphanumeric only).

Front Door SKU the WAF policy attaches to. MUST match the Front Door profile SKU.
Managed rule sets (OWASP/DRS + Bot Manager) are only available on Premium.
WAF enforcement mode. Prevention blocks malicious traffic; Detection only logs.

properties.managedRules: [if(variables('enableManagedRules'), createObject('managedRuleSets', createArray(createObject('ruleSetType', 'Microsoft_DefaultRuleSet', 'ruleSetVersion', '2.1', 'ruleSetAction', 'Block', 'ruleGroupOverrides', createArray(), 'exclusions', createArray()), createObject('ruleSetType', 'Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet', 'ruleSetVersion', '1.0', 'ruleGroupOverrides', createArray(), 'exclusions', createArray()))), createObject('managedRuleSets', createArray()))]

metadata.description: Resource ID of the WAF policy, used to associate it with a Front Door endpoint.
Whether managed OWASP/Bot rule sets were enabled (Premium only).
Globally unique Front Door profile name
Globally unique endpoint name
Backend host name (App Service default hostname)
Log Analytics workspace resource ID for diagnostics

Front Door SKU. Premium is required for WAF managed rule sets (OWASP DRS + Bot
Manager). Standard supports custom WAF rules (rate limiting) only.
Resource ID of the WAF policy to attach to the endpoint. Empty skips WAF.
Front Door instance GUID — used to lock the App Service origin to THIS Front Door via the X-Azure-FDID header.
Name of the Key Vault
Principal ID of the managed identity to grant access
Name of the Azure Container Registry
Principal ID of the managed identity to grant access
Front Door instance GUID. CI uses this to add an X-Azure-FDID access restriction so the App Service origin only accepts traffic from THIS Front Door.
Whether Premium WAF managed rule sets (OWASP DRS + Bot Manager) are active.
